mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE > 4.5%, or the signal fails to separate bull and bear periods with statistical significance at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand across consumption and investment channels, boosting corporate revenue forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >50% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, a sustained increase in the industrial production index reflects rapid capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term equity repricing and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative 5-day returns in >55% of recovery-phase observations, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.5 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls directly expand household income and aggregate consumption capacity, supporting corporate earnings growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of months with positive payroll revisions, or the correlation between payroll growth and 20-day returns drops below 0.10 in rolling 5-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates beyond a 0.3% monthly threshold, it forces markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold signal generates positive forward returns in >55% of exceedance events, or predictive accuracy fails to exceed a random baseline in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms synchronized demand expansion across macroeconomic aggregates and consumer spending, reinforcing equity valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative returns in >50% of aligned periods, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion environments, elevated industrial production levels indicate capacity constraints and overheating risks, prompting profit-taking and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal yields positive 5-day returns in >60% of late-cycle observations, or the regime-conditional predictive power vanishes when tested against a flat benchmark over 36 months.
